concerns.text: Tasks T012-T016 implement single-dataset ingestion (Cyberbullying Survey only), completely omitting the GSS 2022 ingestion, variable harmonization, and propensity-score matching required by spec FR-001 and FR-002. This is a silent relaxation of the spec's core data requirement.

Task T016 saves a 'synthetic cohort' but the implementation logic (single-dataset) does not perform the matching/weighting required to create one. This violates the spec's definition of the 'Synthetic Cohort Unit' (Key Entities) and the acceptance criteria in US-1 (balance check ≤ 0.1).

Success Criterion SC-001 requires measuring covariate balance (standardized mean difference ≤ 0.1) resulting from the synthetic cohort creation. Since the tasks abandon the matching process, there is no task to perform this balance check, rendering SC-001 untestable.

The plan explicitly deviates from spec FR-001/FR-002 due to methodological concerns, but the tasks do not reflect a 'staged' or 'MVP' version of the spec; they simply ignore the requirements. Per FR-030 and the lens rules, tasks cannot silently relax spec demands without explicit spec amendment or documented staging.
